_note: Distilled from the user's creative-strategy skill stack (~/.agents/skills). Data only; method guidance lives in prompt-fragments.js. Card dimensions draw their option sets from here.

awarenessStages.name: Unaware
awarenessStages.who: Doesn't know they have a problem
awarenessStages.strategy: Introduce the pain/desire through a relatable situation or unexpected observation. No product mention.

awarenessStages.name: Problem-Aware
awarenessStages.who: Knows the problem, hasn't found a solution
awarenessStages.strategy: Agitate the pain hard. Make them feel deeply understood. Build urgency.

awarenessStages.name: Solution-Aware
awarenessStages.strategy: Differentiate. Position against alternatives. Call out what's failed them before.

awarenessStages.name: Product-Aware
awarenessStages.strategy: Remove objections. Trigger FOMO. Social proof. Counter the reason they haven't bought.

awarenessStages.name: Most-Aware
awarenessStages.who: Ready to buy, needs a nudge
awarenessStages.strategy: Direct offer, urgency, guarantee, price anchoring. CTA-forward.

mechanics.name: Implied Answer
mechanics.move: Hook poses a question; visuals silently answer it. The viewer concludes the truth themselves.

mechanics.name: Social Witness
mechanics.move: A third party notices the change and comments — validation, not a self-reported claim.

mechanics.name: Overheard Conversation
mechanics.move: Framed as a text thread / DM / group chat you weren't meant to see. Feels like eavesdropping, not an ad.

mechanics.name: Reframe
mechanics.move: Validates a belief the viewer holds, then flips the frame entirely. The new perspective is the hero.

mechanics.name: Borrowed Enemy
mechanics.move: Describes a problem obviously caused by a competitor without naming them. The viewer makes the connection.

mechanics.name: Trojan Horse
mechanics.move: Opens as pure content until the final 20%, where the product appears naturally as the resolution.

mechanics.name: Contrast Without Comment
mechanics.move: Shows two realities side by side with zero editorializing. The visual makes it obvious.

mechanics.name: This and a…
mechanics.move: Pairs the product with something aspirational; meaning is made through association, not explanation.

triggers.name: Pattern Interrupt
triggers.use: Break mental autopilot — say something unexpected, counterintuitive, or visually jarring.

triggers.name: Identity Call-Out
triggers.use: Make the right people self-select with a hyper-specific identity.

triggers.name: Pain Agitation
triggers.use: Mirror their internal monologue so they feel deeply understood.

triggers.name: Curiosity Gap
triggers.use: Open a loop they need to close.

triggers.name: Social Proof / Credibility
triggers.use: Real people, real results, real specificity — numbers, before/afters.

triggers.name: Contrarian / Myth-Busting
triggers.use: Challenge a belief; make them feel they've been lied to.

triggers.name: Aspiration / Desire
triggers.use: Show the version of themselves on the other side of the product.

voicePatterns.name: Confession & Gatekeeping
voicePatterns.job: "I know something you don't" — insider secret energy.

voicePatterns.name: Confusion & Discovery
voicePatterns.job: "Wait, how did I not know this?"

voicePatterns.name: Experience Arc & Progression
voicePatterns.job: "I learned the hard way so you don't have to."

voicePatterns.name: Cultural & Trend Commentary
voicePatterns.job: "Something in the world just revealed a bigger truth."

voicePatterns.name: Explanation Structures
voicePatterns.job: "Let me tell you exactly why this is the way it is."

voicePatterns.name: TikTok Native Formats
voicePatterns.job: Borrowed cultural containers that feel native to the feed.

constraintCards.label: ≤6 words
constraintCards.instruction: The tagline must be 6 words or fewer.

constraintCards.label: As a confession
constraintCards.instruction: Frame the line as a candid personal confession the customer would whisper, not announce.

constraintCards.label: No adjectives
constraintCards.instruction: Use zero adjectives. Let nouns and verbs carry the weight.

constraintCards.label: Ask a question
constraintCards.instruction: The hook must be a question the persona can't stop thinking about.

constraintCards.label: Name a moment
constraintCards.instruction: Anchor the line to one specific moment or time of day in the persona's life.

constraintCards.label: Use a number
constraintCards.instruction: Work a concrete, credible number into the line.

constraintCards.label: No brand name
constraintCards.instruction: Do not use the brand or product name anywhere in the line.

constraintCards.label: Contrarian flip
constraintCards.instruction: Open by validating a common belief, then flip it.

constraintCards.label: Text from a friend
constraintCards.instruction: Make it read like a casual text a friend would actually send.

constraintCards.label: Punchy & plain
constraintCards.instruction: Prefer short, plain, mostly one- and two-syllable words. No jargon.

constraintCards.label: Make it sensory
constraintCards.instruction: Lead with a physical sensation the persona feels in their body.
